title: Multi-Character 4D Mesh Interactions from a Single Monocular Video
teaser: Current mesh animation pipelines focus on single characters, leaving a gap in generating realistic multi-character interactions with consistent per-character topology. This project introduces a 4D framework that predicts per-character vertex-level deformation fields guided by geometric and motion priors (DepthAnythingV3, optical flow, TrellisV2), while enforcing collision-penalty losses and intersection tests for physically plausible close-contact dynamics.
abstract: Current mesh animation pipelines primarily focus on single-character motions, leaving a significant gap in generating realistic multi-character interactions that maintain consistent per-character topology. This project introduces a 4D animation framework for multi-character interactions that maintains temporally consistent, per-character topologies. It achieves this by predicting individual, vertex-level deformation fields optimized with geometric and motion priors (DepthAnythingV3, optical flow, TrellisV2). To ensure physically plausible close-contact dynamics and prevent unnatural mesh inter-penetration, the pipeline explicitly enforces spatial constraints using collision-penalty losses and continuous intersection tests.
milestones: Curate a foundational 3D animation dataset by extracting and processing single-character assets from the Objaverse-XL dataset.
Formulate and optimize a per-character deformation field for single-entity animations, driven by depth, optical flow, and shape priors.
Extend the pipeline to support multi-character interactions, integrating robust physical constraints (e.g., mesh intersection and collision tests) to ensure realistic dynamics.

prerequisites.label: Advanced Deep Learning for Computer Vision: Visual Computing (ADL4CV)

references.label: TRELLIS.2: Native and Compact Structured Latents for 3D Generation
Mesh4D: 4D Mesh Reconstruction and Tracking from Monocular Video
V2M4: 4D Mesh Animation Reconstruction from a Single Monocular Video
